pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 18-19 create the file at this exact path
task.json description explicitly states 'Change it to your local Matomo instance: http://localhost/'; export_result.sh line 26 validates for localhost URL in tracking code
task.json description states 'Change it from 99 to 1'; setup_task.sh line 14 creates 'Initial Site' which is site_id 1; export_result.sh line 31 validates setSiteId to 1
task.json description states 'Add _paq.push(["enableHeartBeatTimer", 10]);'; export_result.sh line 45 validates enableHeartBeatTimer with value 10
setup_task.sh line 25 contains <title>Summer Sale Landing Page</title>; setup_task.sh line 80 queries matomo database for page name 'Summer Sale Landing Page' in matomo_log_link_visit_action table

privileged_info_summary: The task is a Matomo analytics tracking code repair scenario. All metadata values are correct: the target HTML file is at /home/ga/Documents/landing_page.html; the expected tracker URL is http://localhost/ pointing to a local Matomo instance; the expected site ID is 1 (Initial Site); the expected heartbeat timer value is 10 seconds; and the expected page title is 'Summer Sale Landing Page'. The task data is completely synthetic, generated by setup_task.sh which creates the HTML file and Matomo site. Matomo is a self-hosted web analytics platform used in this controlled environment.
